This family consists of approximately 72 genera and 800 species. Amerimnon pseudosissoo is native to Central and South America, and its classification within the family Anacardiaceae is based on its morphological characteristics. The genus Amerimnon is closely related to the genus Schinopsis, and both are classified as part of the tribe Schinantheae. Further classification of Amerimnon pseudosissoo at the species level is still under review.
